## Acceptance Criteria

REQ-12 is implemented: `.jit/gates.json:193` now runs `jit validate "$JIT_ISSUE_ID"`, and gate execution sets `JIT_ISSUE_ID` in `crates/jit/src/gate_execution.rs:53`. The CLI path for `jit validate <id>` reaches `executor.run_rules(id.as_deref())` in `crates/jit/src/main.rs:4583-4588`, which uses the single-issue `run_rules(Some(id))` branch in `crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:944-982`.

I found no built-in `jit-validate` preset under `crates/jit/src/gate_presets`; the only active checker definition for this gate is the registry entry in `.jit/gates.json`.

## Architecture And Separation

Pass. The issue commit `a5c847ab` changes only `.jit/gates.json`; it does not introduce domain/storage/command/CLI boundary movement.

## Functional Paradigm

Pass. No Rust implementation logic was changed for this issue.

## Correctness And Invariants

The gate-scoping behavior is correct for the issue under review. No atomic-write, event-log, gate-transition, assignee/label-format, or PID-safety regression was introduced by the config change.

## Safety

Pass. No unsafe code was introduced.

## Testing

Fail. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed. However, because the provided run history is empty and there is no succeeded `cargo-ci` gate in the issue context, I ran `CARGO_CI_TMPDIR=/tmp/jit-cargo-ci-tmp ./scripts/cargo-ci.sh`. It passed fmt and clippy but failed the workspace test step with 5 failing tests.

## Error Handling

Pass. No new fallible Rust operations were added.

## CLI Contract

Pass. No new CLI command was added, and the existing `jit validate <id>` path supports `--json` in `crates/jit/src/main.rs:4589-4598`.

## Documentation

Fail. One active gate prompt still describes `jit-validate` as repository-wide after this change makes it issue-scoped.

## Prior Review Feedback

`run_history` is empty, so there were no prior findings to verify.

## Jit Issue Management

The listed dependency `7f709152` is `done` in the supplied issue context. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed.

## Findings

1. `./scripts/cargo-ci.sh` fails the workspace test step: `commands::issue::tests::test_claim_issue_enforces_prechecks`, `commands::issue::tests::test_claim_issue_succeeds_when_prechecks_pass`, and three `commands::serve` tests fail. Per the review instructions, any current test failure is blocking even if pre-existing.
2. `scripts/breakdown-review-prompt.md:36` still says repository `jit validate` greenness is enforced by the separate `jit-validate` gate. That is now false because `.jit/gates.json:193` scopes `jit-validate` to `JIT_ISSUE_ID`, leaving an active operational prompt with stale gate semantics.

Total findings: 2

VERDICT: FAIL
